description: The **AWSSupport-TroubleshootEKSDNSFailure** automation runbook helps troubleshoot issues with CoreDNS pods and configuration in Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) when applications/pods are encountering DNS resolution issues. The EKS cluster's authentication mode must be `API` or `API_AND_CONFIG_MAP`.

AutomationAssumeRole.description: (Optional) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) role that allows Systems Manager Automation to perform the actions on your behalf. If no role is specified, Systems Manager Automation uses the permissions of the user that starts this runbook.

EksClusterName.description: (Required) The name of the target Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) cluster.

DnsName.description: (Optional) The stub domain suffix for the domain names that the application or pod fails to resolve. The default is amazon.com.

ProblematicNodeInstanceId.description: (Optional) The instance ID of worker node where application is experiencing DNS resolution errors is running. A value for this parameter means that an EC2 instance will be created in your account and used to perform DNS resolution checks instead of this being done on the worker node. Consider providing a value for this parameter if the worker node is not in a public subnet (or does not have internet access) and do not want or cannot have bind-utils installed on the worker node due to security policies.

CoreDnsNamespace.description: (Optional) The Kubernetes (K8s) namespace for CoreDNS pods. The default value is `kube-system`.

S3BucketName.description: (Optional) The target Amazon S3 bucket name where the troubleshooting logs will be uploaded.

LambdaRoleArn.description: (Optional) The ARN of the IAM role that allows the AWS Lambda function to access the required AWS services and resources. If no role is specified, this Systems Manager Automation will create one IAM role for Lambda in your account with the name `Automation-K8sProxy-Role-<ExecutionId>` that includes the managed policies: `AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole` and `AWSLambdaVPCAccessExecutionRole`. It is recommended that you provide your own role with these required permissions.

checksOnNodes.description: The boolean variable that indicates if checks have been performed on worker nodes.

cfnStackDeleted.description: The boolean variable that indicates if the CloudFormation stack has been deleted.

eksClusterExists.description: The boolean variable that indicates if the EKS cluster exists.

K8sInfrastructureDeleted.description: The boolean variable that indicates if the proxy Lambda function has been deleted.

mainSteps.name: AssertIfTargetClusterExists
mainSteps.description: Asserts if the provided target EKS Cluster specified in the `EksClusterName` parameter exists and is in the `ACTIVE` state.

mainSteps.name: UpdateEksClusterExists
mainSteps.description: Updates the boolean value for the `eksClusterExists` variable.

mainSteps.name: GetVpcDnsSettings
mainSteps.description: Retrieves VPC DNS settings (enableDnsSupport and enableDnsHostnames) for the EKS cluster VPC.

mainSteps.name: BranchOnVpcDnsSettings
mainSteps.description: Checks if VPC DNS settings are properly configured. If either enableDnsSupport or enableDnsHostnames is false, skips to report generation.

mainSteps.name: DeployK8sAuthApisResources
mainSteps.description: Executes the SAW Document `AWSSupport-SetupK8sApiProxyForEKS` to deploy a Lambda function as a proxy to make authenticated API calls.

mainSteps.name: RetrieveCoreDNSDeployment
mainSteps.description: Retrieves information about the CoreDNS pods deployment, the readiness of CoreDNS pods and the containers in these pods and the readiness of the nodes hosting CoreDNS pods.

mainSteps.name: RetrieveAndInspectCoreDNSConfigMap
mainSteps.description: Retrieves the CoreDNS ConfigMap from the EKS cluster and checks for issues in the CoreDNS ConfigMap configuration.

mainSteps.name: ValidateHpaConfiguration
mainSteps.description: Checks if there is HPA configuration for CoreDNS.

mainSteps.name: CheckS3BucketPublicStatus
mainSteps.description: Checks if the Amazon S3 bucket specified in `S3BucketName` allows anonymous, or public read or write access permissions.

mainSteps.name: CollectLogToS3
mainSteps.description: Collect CoreDNS logs and upload them to the S3 bucket.

mainSteps.name: BranchOnProblematicNodeInstanceId
mainSteps.description: Checks if a value for the `ProblematicNodeInstanceId` parameter is provided.

mainSteps.name: VerifyThatProblematicNodeInstanceBelongsToCluster
mainSteps.description: Checks if the `ProblematicNodeInstanceId` belongs to the EKS Cluster.

mainSteps.name: BranchOnProblematicNodeInstanceStanding
mainSteps.description: Branches based on whether the problematic instance belongs to the cluster.

mainSteps.name: GetProblematicInstanceDetails
mainSteps.description: Retrieves details of the problematic instance for probing instance creation.

mainSteps.name: CreateProbingInfrastructure
mainSteps.description: Creates the instance profile and probing EC2 instance via a CloudFormation stack.

inputs.TemplateBody:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: Template to create the instance profile and probing EC2 instance.

Parameters:
  ImageId:
    Type: String
    Description: AMI ID for the probing instance
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Description: Instance type for the probing instance
  SubnetId:
    Type: String
    Description: Subnet ID for the probing instance
  SecurityGroupIds:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Description: Security group IDs for the probing instance
  TagKey:
    Type: String
    Description: Tag key for the probing instance
  TagValue:
    Type: String
    Description: Tag value for the probing instance

Resources:
  ProbingEC2InstanceProfileRole:
    Type: 'AWS::IAM::Role'
    Properties:
      RoleName: ProbingEC2InstanceProfileRole
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Action:
              - 'sts:AssumeRole'
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
      Path: /
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - !Sub 'arn:${AWS::Partition}:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore'

  ProbingEC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: 'AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile'
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: ProbingEC2InstanceProfile
      Path: /
      Roles: 
        - !Ref ProbingEC2InstanceProfileRole

  ProbingEC2Instance:
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::Instance'
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref ImageId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref ProbingEC2InstanceProfile
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroupIds: !Ref SecurityGroupIds
      MetadataOptions:
        HttpTokens: required
        HttpPutResponseHopLimit: 2
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: 20
            VolumeType: gp3
            Encrypted: true
            DeleteOnTermination: true
      Tags:
        - Key: !Ref TagKey
          Value: !Ref TagValue

Outputs:
  ProbingEC2InstanceProfileArn:
    Description: Arn of the instance profile for the probing EC2 instance
    Value: !GetAtt ProbingEC2InstanceProfile.Arn
  ProbingEC2InstanceId:
    Description: Instance ID of the probing EC2 instance
    Value: !Ref ProbingEC2Instance

mainSteps.name: GetProbingInstanceId
mainSteps.description: Retrieves the probing EC2 instance ID from CloudFormation stack outputs.

mainSteps.name: WaitForProbingInstanceSSMAgentStateToBeOnline
mainSteps.description: Waits for the status of the SSM Agent to be `Online` on the probing EC2 instance(s).

mainSteps.name: RetrieveCoreDNSPodsIPFromProblematicNode
mainSteps.description: Retrieves the CoreDNS pods IP from the worker node whose ID is provided with the `ProblematicNodeInstanceId` parameter.

mainSteps.name: PerformDNSResolutionOnProbingEC2Instance
mainSteps.description: Performs domain name resolution on the probing EC2 instance(s) using the cluster IP and CoreDNS pod IP.

commands: echo "       Failed: Name resolution because there are no CoreDNS pods in the problematic node."
# Function to check if yum is locked
# Wait for yum lock to be released
sleep 5  # Adjust the sleep duration as needed
## 2. Check nslookup kubernetes.default with CoreDNS pod IP.
echo "2. Checking nslookup kubernetes.default with CoreDNS pod IP."
echo "       Failed: Name resolution failed for kubernetes.default using CoreDNS IP."
echo "       Passed: Name resolution succeeded for kubernetes.default using CoreDNS IP."
## 3. Check nslookup amazon.com domain with CoreDNS pod IP.
echo "3. Checking nslookup amazon.com domain with CoreDNS pod IP."
echo "       Failed: Name resolution failed for external domain (amazon.com) using CoreDNS IP."
echo "       Passed: Name resolution succeeded for external domain (amazon.com) using CoreDNS IP."
## 3. Check nslookup on stub domain with CoreDNS pod IP.
echo "3. Checking nslookup on stub domain with CoreDNS pod IP."
echo "       Failed: Name resolution failed for stub domain using CoreDNS IP."
echo "       Passed: Name resolution succeeded for stub domain using CoreDNS IP."

mainSteps.name: DeleteCloudFormationStack
mainSteps.description: Deletes the AWS CloudFormation stack that created the instance profile and probing EC2 instance.

mainSteps.name: UpdateCfnStackDeleted
mainSteps.description: Updates the boolean value for the `cfnStackDeleted` variable.

mainSteps.name: BranchOnCoreDnsDeployment
mainSteps.description: Checks if a value for the `ProblematicNodeInstanceId` parameter is not provided and that CoreDNS host nodes exist.

mainSteps.name: BranchOnCoreDnsNodesExistForRunCommandSteps
mainSteps.description: Checks if CoreDNS nodes exist before executing aws:runCommand steps. This prevents execution failures when CoreDNS has 0 replicas and there are no worker nodes to target.

mainSteps.name: PerformDNSResolutionOnCoreDnsWorkerNodes
mainSteps.description: Performs domain name resolution on the worker node using the cluster IP and CoreDNS pod IP.

commands: ## 1. Get the required parameters from kubelet config.
# Function to check if yum is locked
# Wait for yum lock to be released
sleep 5  # Adjust the sleep duration as needed
## 2. Check nslookup kubernetes.default with Cluster IP.
echo "2. Checking nslookup kubernetes.default with Cluster IP."
echo "       Failed: Name resolution failed for kubernetes.default using ClusterIP."
echo "       Passed: Name resolution succeeded for kubernetes.default using ClusterIP."
## 3. Check nslookup on external domain name with Cluster IP.
echo "3. Checking nslookup on amazon.com with Cluster IP."
echo "       Failed: Name resolution failed for external domain (amazon.com) using ClusterIP."
echo "       Passed: Name resolution succeeded for external domain (amazon.com) using ClusterIP."
## 4. Check nslookup kubernetes.default with CoreDNS pod IP.
echo "4. Checking nslookup on kubernetes.default with CoreDNS pod IP."
echo "       Failed: Name resolution failed for kubernetes.default using CoreDNS IP."
echo "       Passed: Name resolution succeeded for kubernetes.default using CoreDNS IP."
## 5. Check nslookup external domain with CoreDNS pod IP.
echo "5. Checking nslookup on amazon.com with CoreDNS pod IP."
echo "       Failed: Name resolution failed for external domain (amazon.com) using CoreDNS IP."
echo "       Passed: Name resolution succeeded for external domain (amazon.com) using CoreDNS IP."
## 6. Check nslookup on stub domain domain with CoreDNS pod IP.
echo "6. Checking nslookup on stub domain domain with CoreDNS pod IP."
echo "       Failed: Name resolution failed for stub domain using CoreDNS IP."
echo "       Passed: Name resolution succeeded for stub domain using CoreDNS IP."

mainSteps.name: VerifyNameserverMatchAndKubeProxyLogsAndIPTableEntries
mainSteps.description: Verifies if the nameserver IP matches the Cluster IP and if kube-proxy pods have access to API Server and if entries in kube-dns are valid.

commands: ## 1. Get the required parameters from kubelet config.
echo "1. Checking kube-proxy logs to see if API server access is valid."
echo "       Failed: Kube-proxy access to API server is not valid."
echo "       Passed: Kube-proxy access to API server is valid."
echo "2. Checking if iptables nat table rules are valid."
echo "    KUBE DNS ENDPOINTS FOUND IN NAT TABLE: [${IPTABLES_SERVICE_KUBEDNS_ENDPOINTS[*]}]"
echo "    The kube-dns endpoint is valid in NAT table: ${i}"
echo "       Failed: There is a problem with the iptables nat table."
echo "       Passed: There is not any problems with the iptables nat table."
echo "3. Checking if nameserver matches ClusterIP."
echo "       Failed: nameserver does not match ClusterIP."

mainSteps.name: VerifyPPSThrottlingOnENIs
mainSteps.description: Verifies the DNS PPS limit per ENI on the worker nodes.

commands: # Function to check if yum is locked
# Wait for yum lock to be released
sleep 5  # Adjust the sleep duration as needed
### 1. By default, the EKS-optimized AMI doesn't include ethtool. Install the tool
echo "Skip to check the loopback device" > /dev/null 2>&1
### 3. Check the interface is ENA enabled and support monitoring metrics
IS_USING_ENA_DRIVER=$(ethtool -i $interface | grep -q "driver: ena" && echo true || echo false)
echo "$interface is not ENA enabled, will skip to check this interface\n"
### 4. Print the ENA driver version. It should be 2.2.10 or later
### 5. Checking if the interface (ENI) exceeded the limit
### Use RegExp to check [1-9]+ matches one or more digits from 1 to 9, but not the digit 0. So if any of the counters have a value of exactly 0, they will be ignored by the script.
echo "    Failed: At least one counter for $interface has exceeded its allowance."
echo "       Passed: All counters for $interface are within their allowances."
### 6. Remove the ethtool at the end of this step because customer may have any security compliance tool will inspect unwanted packages.

mainSteps.name: UpdateChecksOnNodes
mainSteps.description: Updates the boolean value for the `checksOnNodes` variable.

mainSteps.name: CleanupK8sAuthenticationInfrastructure
mainSteps.description: Executes the SAW Document `AWSSupport-SetupK8sApiProxyForEKS` using the `Cleanup` operation to clean up resources created as part of the automation.

mainSteps.name: UpdateK8sInfrastructureDeleted
mainSteps.description: Updates the boolean value for the `K8sInfrastructureDeleted` variable.

mainSteps.name: CleanUpAllResources
mainSteps.description: Comprehensively cleans up resources left behind from the preceding steps.

mainSteps.name: CollectOutputFromAllRunCommandSteps
mainSteps.description: Collects output from all run command steps that have been executed.

mainSteps.name: GenerateReport
mainSteps.description: Compiles the output of the previous steps and outputs a report.
